claim: LangGraph CLI enables scaffolding, local testing, and production deployment from terminal with single commands
actionable: Use 'langgraph new', 'langgraph dev', and 'langgraph deploy' to streamline agent development workflow from prototype to production
source_title: Deploy CLI: The Easiest Way to Deploy Agents

claim: LangSmith Studio enables hot-reload of agent code, allowing real-time iteration without redeployment during local testing
actionable: Use Studio's hot-reload feature during local development to rapidly test prompt changes and agent logic modifications
source_title: Deploy CLI: The Easiest Way to Deploy Agents

claim: Every LangGraph deployment comes with MCP and AT protocol support out of the box for tool integration
actionable: Use standard protocols (MCP, AT) when building external tool integrations with LangGraph deployments
source_title: Deploy CLI: The Easiest Way to Deploy Agents

claim: LangGraph deployments provide versioned configurations (assistants) allowing A/B testing of agent variants in production
actionable: Create multiple assistant configurations in LangGraph to test prompt variations, model choices, or tool combinations against users
source_title: Deploy CLI: The Easiest Way to Deploy Agents

claim: Agent traces in LangSmith show execution flow, tool calls, and reasoning paths for debugging and monitoring
actionable: Review agent traces in LangSmith to identify inefficient tool calls, reasoning loops, or unexpected behaviors
source_title: Deploy CLI: The Easiest Way to Deploy Agents
